about_cvsu: Cavite State University (CvSU) traces its roots to 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School, with the American Thomasites as the first teachers. It was renamed Indang Farm School in 1918, Indang Rural High School in 1927, and Don Severino National Agriculture School in 1958. In 1964, the school was converted into a State College via Republic Act 3917 and became known as Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC). On January 22, 1998, by virtue of Republic Act No. 8468, DSAC was converted into Cavite State University. In 2001, the Cavite College of Fisheries (CACOF) in Naic and the Cavite College of Arts and Trade (CCAT) in Rosario were integrated into the University via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. At present, CvSU has 11 campuses in the province; the main campus — the Don Severino delas Alas Campus in Indang, Cavite — is approximately 70 hectares in land area. The University offers close to 100 curricular programs in the undergraduate and graduate levels, with more than 25,000 students and 1,200 faculty and staff from all campuses. CvSU celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ The main campus is named after Don Severino de las Alas — an educator, public servant, and philanthropist who donated a portion of his land for the establishment of the intermediate school that became CvSU. The University is led by President Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro. It is accredited by TESDA as a Trade and Testing Venue, designated by the Department of Agriculture as the National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture, and hosts the Southern Tagalog Agriculture Research and Development Consortium (STARDEC), the Affiliated Renewable Energy Center for Region IV-A, and the Regional ICT Center for Region IV-A. CvSU holds AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III (recognized as the second top performing state university with the highest number of Level III accredited programs), is ISO 9001:2015 certified, ranked 217th in the WURI 2026 Top 500 Global Innovative Universities, and recently received its 5th IPOPHL Platinum Award and first Palladium Award — the first higher education institution in the Southern Tagalog Region to do so — plus top eco-friendly school honors from the DENR-EMB.

CvSU started in 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School (under the American Thomasites). It went through several name changes — Indang Farm School (1918), Indang Rural High School (1927), Don Severino National Agriculture School (1958) — before becoming a State College in 1964 via RA 3917 (Don Severino Agricultural College, DSAC). On January 22, 1998, RA 8468 converted DSAC into Cavite State University. CACOF (Naic) and CCAT (Rosario) joined CvSU in 2001 via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. Today CvSU has 11 campuses in Cavite, close to 100 curricular programs, and more than 25,000 students — and it celebrated its 120th founding anniversary in January 2026. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ CvSU is led by President Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro, holds AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III, is ISO 9001:2015 certified, ranked 217th in WURI 2026, is the DA-designated National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture, and received its 5th IPOPHL Platinum and first Palladium Award in 2026. The main campus honors Don Severino de las Alas, the educator and philanthropist who donated the land where the school began.

Ang CvSU ay nagsimula noong 1906 bilang Indang Intermediate School, sa panahon ng mga Thomasites. Naging Indang Farm School ito noong 1918, Indang Rural High School noong 1927, Don Severino National Agriculture School noong 1958, at naging State College noong 1964 sa bisa ng RA 3917. Noong Enero 22, 1998, sa bisa ng RA 8468, ito ay naging Cavite State University. Noong 2001, ang CACOF (Naic) at CCAT (Rosario) ay isinama sa CvSU sa pamamagitan ng CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. Sa kasalukuyan, may 11 campus ang CvSU sa lalawigan; ang Main Campus (Don Severino delas Alas Campus, humigit-kumulang 70 ektarya) ay nasa Indang, Cavite. May halos 100 curricular programs at mahigit 25,000 estudyante ang Pamantasan, at ipinagdiwang nito ang ika-120 anibersaryo ng pagkakatatag noong Enero 2026. Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/ Ang pangulo ng CvSU ay si Dr. Ma. Agnes P. Nuestro. Ang Pamantasan ay may AACCUP Institutional Accreditation Level III, ISO 9001:2015 certified, ika-217 sa WURI 2026, itinalagang National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture ng Department of Agriculture, at tumanggap ng ika-5 IPOPHL Platinum Award at kauna-unahang Palladium Award. Ang Main Campus ay ipinangalan kay Don Severino de las Alas — isang guro, lingkod-bayan, at pilantropo na nagkaloob ng bahagi ng kanyang lupain para sa paaralang naging CvSU.
academic_calendar:
CvSU ACADEMIC CALENDAR (general guide — dates change each year):

CvSU follows the CHED academic calendar with a First Semester, Second Semester, and (where applicable) a Summer Term. Each semester typically includes midterm and final examination periods, plus the regular Philippine holidays. The university also observes its own Founding Anniversary and university-wide events such as Sportsfest, Foundation Week, and Graduation.

For the official, current-year academic calendar — including exact dates of classes, exams, holidays, the Founding Day commemoration, and graduation — please:
- Visit https://cvsu.edu.ph (and your specific campus page)
- Follow CvSU's official social media
- Contact your campus registrar

academic_integrity: Academic integrity at CvSU is governed by the University Student Handbook and applicable codes of conduct. Violations include plagiarism, cheating in examinations, contract cheating/ghostwriting, fabrication of data, and unauthorized collaboration. Penalties range from a failing grade in the specific requirement, a failing grade in the subject, to suspension or dismissal for serious or repeated offenses. Reports are typically reviewed by the College Dean and, depending on severity, by a Student Discipline Committee. Always cite sources properly and follow the prescribed citation style of your program.

Plagiarism and cheating at CvSU carry penalties ranging from a failing grade in a requirement to dismissal for serious cases. Review the Student Handbook and follow your program's citation requirements.

Ang academic dishonesty (plagiarism, cheating) sa CvSU ay may parusang nag-uumpisa sa failing grade hanggang dismissal sa malalang kaso. Tingnan ang Student Handbook at gamitin nang tama ang citation.
academic_policies:
CvSU ACADEMIC POLICIES & GRADING SYSTEM:

GRADING SCALE (1.0–5.0):
1.0 = Excellent | 1.25 = Superior | 1.5 = Very Good
1.75 = Good | 2.0 = Satisfactory | 2.25 = Fairly Satisfactory
2.5 = Passing | 2.75 = Conditional Pass | 3.0 = Passed (75%)
5.0 = Failed | INC = Incomplete | W = Withdrawn

MINIMUM PASSING GRADE: 3.0 (75%)

GWA: Weighted average of all course grades × credit units

INCOMPLETE (INC):
- Must be completed within 1 academic year
- Becomes 5.0 (Failed) if not resolved

LEAVE OF ABSENCE (LOA):
- Approved by the Dean; maximum 2 consecutive semesters
- Submit LOA form to the Registrar's Office

SHIFTING:
- Requires Dean's approval; subject to slot availability
- May need to retake non-credited subjects

RETENTION: Varies per college — consult your department for specific GWA requirements.

Contact the Registrar's Office for official grade records and policy clarifications.

Ang passing grade sa CvSU ay 3.0 (75%). Gumagamit ang CvSU ng 1.0–5.0 grading scale. Ang GWA ay kinukuha mula sa weighted average ng lahat ng iyong grades. Ang INC grade ay kailangang ayusin sa loob ng isang taon. Para sa LOA, course shifting, o retention policy, makipag-ugnayan sa iyong Dean at sa Registrar's Office. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
add_drop_subjects: Adding or dropping a subject at CvSU is allowed only within the Add/Drop Period, which is typically the first 1–2 weeks of the semester (exact dates published per semester). To add: secure approval from your adviser and submit the add form. To officially drop: submit a drop form through your adviser and the Registrar — an officially dropped subject is recorded as DRP, not as a failing grade. Dropping AFTER the deadline without filing may result in a 5.0 (Failed) grade. For the current Add/Drop window, check the academic calendar and your campus Registrar.

Adds and drops are processed during the Add/Drop period at the start of each semester. Officially dropped subjects get a DRP code; unofficial drops or drops after the deadline can become 5.0. Coordinate with your adviser and the Registrar.

Ang add/drop ng subject sa CvSU ay sa loob lamang ng Add/Drop Period sa simula ng semester. Ang opisyal na dropped subject ay tinatakan ng DRP, hindi 5.0. Mag-coordinate sa iyong adviser at Registrar.
admissions_exam: The CvSU entrance examination is part of the admission process. After you complete the online application at https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ and submit your documents to OSAS, you will be scheduled for the entrance exam at your target campus. Bring your application form, valid ID, and exam permit/notice as instructed. Successful applicants are issued a Notice of Admission. For the current entrance-exam schedule and admission cycle, please monitor https://cvsu.edu.ph and the admission portal. Source: https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/

After filing your application at admission.cvsu.edu.ph and submitting documents to OSAS, you'll be scheduled for the CvSU entrance examination at your target campus. Bring your application form, valid ID, and exam permit. Notices of Admission are issued to qualified applicants. For the latest schedule, see https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/.

Pagkatapos ng online application sa admission.cvsu.edu.ph at submission sa OSAS, ise-schedule ka para sa CvSU entrance exam. Dalhin ang application form, valid ID, at exam permit. Ang qualified applicants ay tatanggap ng Notice of Admission. Para sa pinakabagong iskedyul, tingnan ang https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/.
admissions_requirements:
ADMISSION TO CvSU (per the official Office of Student Affairs and Services and the admission portal):

1. FILE THE ONLINE APPLICATION at https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ — encode your information in the registration/application form.
2. PRINT the accomplished application form on A4-size bond paper.
3. PREPARE THESE DOCUMENTS (typical for SHS-graduate applicants):
   - Certified true copy of Grade 12 Report Card (Form 138)
   - Certificate of Good Moral Character
   - 2 copies of 1x1 ID picture with name tag
   - Short ordinary folder
4. SUBMIT TO OSAS at your target campus for exam scheduling (typically Mondays–Thursdays, 7 AM–6 PM).
5. TAKE THE CvSU ENTRANCE EXAMINATION.
6. APPLICANTS FOR ARCHITECTURE, COMPUTER SCIENCE, AND ENGINEERING must additionally bring their Grade 11 Report Card for evaluation. Engineering programs accept STEM-strand graduates.
7. BS PSYCHOLOGY applicants require a GPA of 85 or higher.
8. TRANSFEREE applicants must have a GPA of 2.00 (equivalent of 85%) or better with NO failed grade.

For the current cycle's exact requirements and deadlines, see https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ and https://cvsu.edu.ph. Sources: https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ · https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/

Mga pangunahing requirements para sa CvSU admission: mag-online application sa admission.cvsu.edu.ph, i-print sa A4 bond paper, ihanda ang certified true copy ng Grade 12 Report Card, Good Moral Certificate, 2 1x1 ID pictures na may name tag, at short ordinary folder. Isumite sa OSAS ng inyong campus para sa exam scheduling. Para sa Architecture/CS/Engineering, magdala rin ng Grade 11 Report Card (Engineering: STEM strand). BS Psychology: GPA 85+. Transferee: GPA 2.00 (85%) at walang failed grade. Pinagmulan: https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/
agriculture_programs: CvSU offers a range of agriculture-related undergraduate programs through the College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources (CAFENR) — typically including BS Agriculture (with specializations), BS Agricultural and Biosystems Engineering, BS Animal Science, BS Food Technology, BS Forestry/Agroforestry, BS Agribusiness Management, and related programs. Notably, BS Food Technology (CAFENR) holds AACCUP Level IV re-accredited status (2026), and CvSU is designated by the Department of Agriculture as the National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture. Availability varies per campus. For the current program list and the campuses that offer each, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU's agriculture cluster, under CAFENR (College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources), typically offers BS Agriculture (with specializations), BS Agricultural Engineering, BS Animal Science, BS Food Technology (AACCUP Level IV re-accredited, 2026), BS Agroforestry, and BS Agribusiness. CvSU is also the DA-designated National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture. Availability varies per campus.

Ang CvSU ay nag-aalok ng mga agriculture-related programs sa ilalim ng CAFENR (College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources): BS Agriculture, BS Agricultural Engineering, BS Animal Science, BS Food Technology (AACCUP Level IV re-accredited, 2026), atbp. Ang CvSU rin ang itinalagang National Center for Research and Development for Coffee and Urban Agriculture ng Department of Agriculture. Ang availability ay nag-iiba bawat campus.
als_admission: Yes, CvSU accepts ALS (Alternative Learning System) Accreditation & Equivalency (A&E) passers. As an ALS applicant you generally submit your ALS Certificate of Rating / Diploma in lieu of Form 138, plus the other standard freshman documents (PSA birth certificate, Good Moral, 2x2 photos, application form). You take the same CvSU entrance examination and are evaluated together with other applicants. For the current process and any additional requirements for ALS applicants, please confirm with the Admissions Office through https://cvsu.edu.ph.
CvSU accepts ALS A&E passers. Submit your ALS Certificate of Rating in place of Form 138, plus the standard freshman documents. You'll take the same entrance exam.

anti_bullying_safe_spaces:
CvSU is governed by Philippine laws that protect students from bullying, harassment, and gender-based violence — including the Anti-Bullying Act (RA 10627), the Safe Spaces Act (RA 11313), the Anti-Sexual Harassment Act (RA 7877), and the Anti-VAWC Act (RA 9262). The university has internal policies and committees to receive and act on reports.

IF YOU ARE EXPERIENCING OR HAVE WITNESSED BULLYING / HARASSMENT / GBV at CvSU:
- Document what happened (date, place, people involved, what was said/done) if it is safe to do so.
- Report to the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), your Guidance Office, or the campus Committee on Decorum and Investigation (CODI) / Gender and Development (GAD) Office — whichever your campus designates as the receiving channel.
- You may also seek support from the Guidance and Counseling Office.

For immediate danger, call 911 or local authorities. For the verified reporting channel at your campus, please refer to https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Sumusunod ang CvSU sa Anti-Bullying Act (RA 10627), Safe Spaces Act (RA 11313), Anti-Sexual Harassment Act (RA 7877), at Anti-VAWC Act (RA 9262). Maaaring iulat ang insidente sa OSAS, Guidance Office, o sa CODI/GAD ng inyong campus. Para sa agarang panganib, tumawag ng 911.
audit_subject: Auditing a subject (attending classes without earning credit) may be allowed at CvSU at the instructor's discretion and with the approval of the Dean. Audit students typically don't take exams or receive a grade, and the subject doesn't count toward graduation. Specific rules — including any fees and whether your status (current student, alumnus, non-student) qualifies — vary per campus. Inquire with the Department Chair and the Registrar.
Auditing classes (no credit, no grade) is at the instructor and Dean's discretion. Fees and eligibility vary — verify with the Department and Registrar.
Ang pag-audit ng subject (walang credit, walang grado) ay maaari ngunit nasa pag-apruba ng instructor at Dean. Magtanong sa Department at Registrar.
authentication_apostille: If you need CvSU documents authenticated for use abroad, the typical path is: (1) request the document (TOR / Diploma / Good Moral) from the CvSU Registrar; (2) obtain a Certification, Authentication, and Verification (CAV) from CHED for tertiary documents; (3) apostille the authenticated document at the DFA (the apostille has replaced the old 'red ribbon' under the Apostille Convention since 2019). Processing fees and times apply at each step. Plan ahead — the full chain can take weeks. For the current CHED/DFA process, check the agencies' official websites; for CvSU document fees, check with the Registrar.
Documents-for-abroad chain: CvSU Registrar → CHED CAV → DFA apostille. Apostille replaced the old red ribbon in 2019. Plan ahead — the full chain can take weeks.
Para sa dokumentong gagamitin sa ibang bansa: CvSU Registrar → CHED CAV → DFA apostille. Ang apostille ang pumalit sa 'red ribbon' simula 2019. Magbalak nang maaga.
board_exam_review: CvSU graduates of board-program courses take their licensure examinations under the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC), and CvSU has a strong recent record — per the official site's 2026 results: top performing school in the February 2026 Criminologist Licensure Exam (Main 99.25%, Bacoor 100%); Top 10 (Elementary, Main Campus, 95.06%) and Top 3 (Secondary, Imus Campus, 98.36%) performing schools in the March 2026 LEPT with six topnotchers; 7th top performing school in the March 2026 Electronics Engineers Licensure Exam (82.14%); 97.33% in the March 2026 Medical Technologists exam; 95.56% in the June 2026 Architects exam (vs 85.08% national) and 100% in the January 2026 run; and top 10 performing school in the February 2026 Master Plumbers exam (83.33%). Some colleges offer in-house review classes or partner with accredited review centers; others let graduates choose external review centers. For your college's review program, contact your Department Chair. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph

Board exam reviews at CvSU vary by program — some colleges offer in-house review; others partner with external review centers. CvSU's recent 2026 results (per the official site) include: top performing school for Criminologists (Feb 2026), Top 3/Top 10 LEPT performances with six topnotchers (Mar 2026), 7th top performing school for Electronics Engineers (Mar 2026), 97.33% for Medical Technologists (Mar 2026), and 95.56–100% for Architects (2026). Verify review options with your Department Chair after graduation.

business_management: CvSU does not have a college named exactly “College of Business Management and Operations.” Business and management programs — including the Bachelor of Science in Business Management (BSBM) and the Bachelor of Science in Business Administration (BSBA) — are offered through the Department of Management under the College of Economics, Management and Development Studies (CEMDS). Per the official site, BS Business Administration at the Carmona and CCAT (Rosario) campuses holds AACCUP Level III re-accredited status (2026). Hospitality and tourism programs (BS Hospitality Management and BS Tourism Management) sit separately under the College of Tourism and Hospitality Management (CTHM), approved as an independent college on September 26, 2024. For the current list of business/management programs and the campuses that offer each, see https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

Business and management programs at CvSU — BSBM (Bachelor of Science in Business Management) and BSBA (Bachelor of Science in Business Administration) — are offered via the Department of Management under CEMDS (College of Economics, Management and Development Studies); there is no college named “College of Business Management and Operations.” BSBA at the Carmona and CCAT campuses is AACCUP Level III re-accredited (2026). Hospitality/tourism programs are under CTHM. See https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/ for the current list.

Walang college sa CvSU na eksaktong tinatawag na “College of Business Management and Operations.” Ang mga business at management programs — kabilang ang BS Business Management (BSBM) at BS Business Administration (BSBA) — ay inaalok sa pamamagitan ng Department of Management sa ilalim ng CEMDS (College of Economics, Management and Development Studies). Ang BSBA sa Carmona at CCAT ay AACCUP Level III re-accredited (2026). Ang hospitality at tourism programs ay nasa ilalim ng CTHM. Tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

campus_location: The CvSU Main Campus — the Don Severino delas Alas Campus, approximately 70 hectares in land area — is located in Indang, Cavite, Philippines, approximately 60 km (about 37 miles) southwest of Manila. At present, CvSU has 11 campuses in the province, with satellite campuses including Bacoor, Cavite City, Carmona, General Trias, Imus, Naic, Rosario (CCAT), Silang, Tanza, and Trece Martires City. choosing_program: Choosing a program is a personal decision — the 'best' course depends on your interests, strengths, financial situation, career goals, and the demand in your field. A useful approach: (1) list your interests and what energizes you to study, (2) check what programs CvSU offers that match, (3) look at the curriculum and the kind of work alumni do, (4) factor in your SHS strand and entrance-exam readiness, (5) consider career outcomes (board exam, employability). CvSU's Guidance Office offers career counseling — they can help you think this through.

counseling_procedure:
OSAS COUNSELING PROCEDURE:

1. Fill out the Counseling Form (online or on-site at OSAS).
2. Undergo initial interview with the Guidance Counselor.
3. Fill out Appointment/Commitment Form.
4. Attend counseling session (face-to-face or online) — 45 min to 1 hour 30 min per session.
5. Fill out Counseling Completion Form. The counselor may follow up later.

WHO MAY AVAIL: All enrolled students.
FEES: None.
TOTAL PROCESSING TIME: ~1 hour 10 minutes.
Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — OSAS, p.554.

Para mag-pa-counseling sa OSAS: punan ang Counseling Form (online o on-site), mag-initial interview kay Guidance Counselor, mag-set ng appointment, dumalo sa session (45 min – 1.5 hours), at i-fill up ang completion form. Libre at para sa lahat ng enrolled students. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.554)

data_privacy: CvSU, as a Philippine state university, is governed by the Data Privacy Act of 2012 (RA 10173). Personal data collected from students and staff (e.g., during admission, enrollment, scholarship application, and document requests) is processed only for legitimate university purposes and protected per the law's requirements. As a data subject, you generally have the right to be informed about how your data is used, to access and correct your data, and to file a complaint. To exercise these rights — or to request access, correction, or deletion of your records — please contact the CvSU Data Protection Officer (DPO) through the verified contact channel on https://cvsu.edu.ph.

emergency_oncampus:
For on-campus emergencies at CvSU:
- Life-threatening emergencies (medical, fire, crime): call 911 (national emergency number) immediately, then notify campus Security and the Health Service Unit.
- Minor medical concerns: go to the Health Service Unit (clinic) at your campus.
- Fire: activate the nearest fire alarm if available, evacuate via marked routes, do not use elevators, and gather at the assembly point.
- Earthquake: drop, cover, hold; evacuate after shaking stops via stairs to the assembly point.
Campus Security desks have direct contact lines to local emergency responders. Save your campus Security and Clinic numbers from the official directory.

Para sa emergency sa CvSU campus: tumawag ng 911 para sa malubha, pumunta sa Health Service Unit para sa hindi mabigat na medical issue, sumunod sa fire/earthquake protocols. I-save ang Security at Clinic numbers ng inyong campus.

For Certificate of Internship Completion, your department's OJT coordinator issues this after you complete the OJT requirements. Specific fees and turnaround vary by document and campus.

Engineering programs accept STEM-strand SHS graduates and require the Grade 11 Report Card for evaluation during admission. enrollment_problems:
Common enrollment problems and where to go:
- Account or portal login issue → ICT/MIS Office at your campus
- Subjects not appearing or unable to select → your adviser, then the Registrar
- Section full / no slots → coordinate with your Dean's Office; petition for a class may be allowed if enough students are affected
- Schedule conflict between subjects → adjust with your adviser; one or both may need to be moved or dropped
- COR not generating after subject selection → Registrar's Office
- Holds on your record (unpaid balance, unreturned books, missing clearance) → settle with the relevant office (Cashier, Library, etc.)
If you're stuck, your first stop is usually your academic adviser, then the Registrar.

Para sa mga problema sa enrollment: login issues → ICT/MIS; problema sa subject o COR → Registrar; punong section → Dean's Office; account holds → ayusin sa kaukulang opisina (Cashier, Library, atbp.). Magsimula sa inyong adviser kung hindi sigurado.
enrollment_procedure:
CvSU ENROLLMENT PROCEDURE:

AFTER ADMISSION (NOA received):

1. PRE-ENROLLMENT:
   - Receive Notice of Admission (NOA)
   - Attend department interview/orientation
   - Complete medical examination
   - Get medical clearance

2. ACADEMIC ADVISING:
   - Meet with department adviser
   - Discuss curriculum and course load
   - Get program of study

3. REGISTRATION:
   - Online enrollment via student portal
   - Select courses for the semester
   - Print Certificate of Registration (COR)

4. PAYMENT (Misc fees only - tuition is FREE under RA 10931):
   - Pay miscellaneous fees at Cashier's Office
   - Get official receipt

5. FINALIZATION:
   - Submit COR to Registrar
   - Get student ID
   - Receive class schedule

FOR CONTINUING STUDENTS:
- Online registration through student portal
- Selection of subjects with adviser approval
- Payment of fees if applicable
- COR submission

CONTACT: For the verified Office of the University Registrar contacts (email and phone) for your campus, please check www.cvsu.edu.ph.

Para sa enrollment sa CvSU: tanggapin ang NOA, dumalo sa departmental orientation, kumpletuhin ang medical exam, mag-advising sa iyong adviser, mag-enroll sa Student Portal, bayaran ang miscellaneous fees sa Cashier (libre ang tuition!), at i-submit ang COR sa Registrar. Para sa continuing students, gamitin ang Student Portal sa enrollment period. Salamat sa inyong tanong!

events: CvSU holds events and activities throughout the academic year, though exact dates change annually. Major annual events include the Founding Anniversary held in late January — CvSU celebrated its 120th on January 28-29, 2026, including the annual Gawad Laya at Diwa and Partners' Recognition Day — along with Foundation Week, the University Sportsfest, cultural festivals, research forums and symposia, the graduation ceremony, and freshman orientation. Academic events include research symposia, college-specific weeks, inter-college competitions, and international conferences hosted at the CvSU International Convention Center, such as the 4th International Conference on Women Empowerment and Leadership in Agriculture, Aquatic and Natural Resources (WELA 2026) on March 17-19, 2026. Athletic competitions include the SCUAA (State Colleges and Universities Athletic Association) meets plus inter-campus games and regional/national tournaments, while community outreach and volunteerism/service initiatives are coordinated under the LINGAP CvSU program of the Extension Services Center. Students can also join academic, cultural, religious, sports, and honor-society student organizations. foreign_student_admission:
FOREIGN STUDENT ADMISSION (OSAS):

REQUIREMENTS:
- Accomplished Application Form for Admission (download at www.cvsu.edu.ph)
- Photocopy of Report Card / Transcript of Records
- Photocopy of transfer credentials
- Photocopy of student visa (passport)
- Police Clearance from country of origin
- Authenticated Affidavit of Financial Support
- 2 pcs 1x1 photo (white background)
- 1 short ordinary folder
- Official receipt for testing fee

FEE: PHP 500 (testing fee, paid at Cashier's Office).
PROCESS:
1. Pay testing fee at Cashier's Office.
2. Submit required documents to OSAS.
3. Receive examination permit and admission exam schedule.
4. Take the admission exam.
5. For BS Nursing, BS Med Tech, Midwifery, BSHM, BSTM, BSA, BS International Studies — interview at the College is required.

Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — OSAS, p.532.

Foreign student admission sa CvSU: magbayad ng PHP 500 testing fee sa Cashier, isumite ang requirements sa OSAS (application form, TOR, visa, police clearance, affidavit of financial support, 2 pcs 1x1, atbp.). Kunin ang exam permit, kumuha ng exam, at kapag BS Nursing / Med Tech / Midwifery / BSHM / BSTM / BSA / BS Intl Studies, may interview. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.532)
free_software_microsoft: CvSU students may have access to Microsoft 365 (Office apps + cloud storage) and/or Google Workspace for Education through the university's institutional subscription, sign-in tied to your @cvsu.edu.ph account. Additional educational licenses (Adobe, Autodesk, etc.) may be offered by specific colleges (e.g., engineering, design programs) for in-classroom use. Specific entitlements and how to claim them vary — contact your campus ICT/MIS Office or your College for the current list.

Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace for Education may be accessible via your @cvsu.edu.ph account. Additional educational software (Adobe, Autodesk, etc.) may come through specific colleges. Check with ICT/MIS and your College.

Maaaring may access ka sa Microsoft 365 at Google Workspace for Education gamit ang iyong @cvsu.edu.ph account. Ang ibang educational software (Adobe, Autodesk) ay maaaring mula sa iyong college. Magtanong sa ICT/MIS.
free_tuition_law_details:
RA 10931 — Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act of 2017 — provides free tuition and other school fees (per the law's implementing rules) for qualified Filipino students in undergraduate programs at state universities and colleges, including CvSU.

GENERAL ELIGIBILITY (verify current rules with UniFAST and your CvSU Cashier/OSAS office):
- Filipino citizen
- Admitted to CvSU
- Enrolled in an undergraduate degree program
- Maintains the academic standing required by the institution and the law
- Has not earned a previous undergraduate degree

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- The Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) is a separate cash subsidy under the same law, processed via UniFAST — priority goes to students from low-income households.
- RA 10931 may not cover graduate programs the same way as undergraduate programs.
- Students may opt out of the free-tuition benefit if they wish.
- Some fees may still apply (replacement IDs, certain personal items, etc.).

For the verified, current implementation at CvSU, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph or contact your campus Cashier/OSAS.

Ang RA 10931 ay nagbibigay ng libreng tuition at iba pang school fees para sa qualified Filipino na undergraduate students sa mga SUC tulad ng CvSU. Kailangan ng Filipino citizenship, admission sa undergraduate program, at pagsunod sa academic standing. Ang TES ay hiwalay na cash subsidy sa ilalim ng parehong batas. Para sa kasalukuyang detalye sa CvSU, makipag-ugnayan sa Cashier o OSAS ng inyong campus.
good_moral_certificate: A Certificate of Good Moral Character from CvSU is issued by the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), or in some campuses by the Guidance Office or Registrar, depending on internal procedure. To request: fill out the form, pay any applicable fee at the Cashier, and claim per the published schedule. Commonly used for transferring to another school, applying for board exams, and employment. Processing time varies — verify with your campus OSAS/Registrar.
Good Moral certificates are issued by OSAS (or the Registrar/Guidance Office depending on campus). File request, pay fee, claim. Used for transfer, board exam, and employment.

Ang Certificate of Good Moral Character ay ibinibigay ng OSAS (o Registrar/Guidance Office). Mag-file ng request, magbayad ng bayad, at kunin. Karaniwang ginagamit sa transfer, board exam, at trabaho.
goodbye: Goodbye! Feel free to come back anytime. Iskolar para sa Bayan!
Take care! Don't hesitate to ask if you need more help with CvSU.
Goodbye! Best of luck with your CvSU journey! Truth, Integrity, Excellence, Service.
grade_dispute: If you believe a grade was recorded incorrectly or was unfair, the standard escalation path at CvSU is: (1) raise the concern with your subject teacher first — minor errors can be fixed by a change-of-grade form filed by the instructor; (2) if unresolved, escalate to the Department Chair; (3) then to the College Dean; (4) finally, to the Registrar if the issue is documentary. Timelines for grade disputes are strict — usually within one semester of the grade being posted. Bring evidence (graded papers, attendance records, submitted work).
Grade disputes escalate teacher → Department Chair → Dean → Registrar. Strict timelines apply (usually within one semester of posting). Bring evidence to support your claim.
Para sa grade dispute: instructor → Department Chair → Dean → Registrar. Mahigpit ang deadline (kadalasang isang semester mula sa pag-post). Magdala ng ebidensya.
graduate_programs: CvSU operates a Graduate School offering master's and doctoral programs, along with the Open Learning College for non-traditional learners (see the OGS/OLC offerings at https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ogs-olc-program-offerings/). Programs span education, agriculture, sciences, management, and other fields. Admission typically requires a bachelor's degree, transcript of records, and program-specific entrance requirements. Note: free tuition under RA 10931 generally does NOT cover graduate programs the same way as undergraduate — please verify applicable fees with the Cashier's Office. For the current list of graduate programs, see https://cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU's Graduate School and Open Learning College (OGS/OLC) offer master's and doctoral programs across various fields. Admission typically requires a bachelor's degree, TOR, and program-specific entrance requirements. RA 10931 may not cover graduate programs the same way — verify with the Cashier's Office. See https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ogs-olc-program-offerings/.

May Graduate School at Open Learning College (OGS/OLC) ang CvSU para sa master's at doctoral programs. Karaniwang kailangan ng bachelor's degree, TOR, at program-specific requirements. Ang RA 10931 ay maaaring hindi sumasaklaw sa graduate programs sa parehong paraan — magtanong sa Cashier's Office. Tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ogs-olc-program-offerings/.
graduate_school_abroad: If you plan to pursue graduate studies abroad after CvSU, the typical preparation includes: (1) good academic standing and an authenticated TOR (CAV + DFA apostille), (2) standardized exams as required by your target country/program (TOEFL/IELTS for English; GRE/GMAT for many graduate programs in the US), (3) recommendation letters from your CvSU professors, (4) a strong statement of purpose, and (5) scholarship application materials (Fulbright, DAAD, Chevening, Australia Awards, Erasmus, country-specific scholarships, etc.). Start research and document preparation 12–18 months ahead of your target intake.

For abroad graduate studies: authenticated TOR (CAV + DFA apostille), standardized exams (TOEFL/IELTS, possibly GRE/GMAT), recommendation letters, statement of purpose, and scholarship apps (Fulbright, DAAD, Chevening, Australia Awards, Erasmus). Plan 12–18 months ahead.

Para sa graduate studies sa ibang bansa pagkatapos ng CvSU: authenticated TOR (CAV + DFA apostille), standardized exams (TOEFL/IELTS, GRE/GMAT), recommendation letters, statement of purpose, at scholarship applications. Maghanda nang 12–18 buwan bago ang target.
graduation_requirements: Graduation at CvSU typically requires: (1) completing all academic units in your curriculum (including any minor/major electives and the NSTP requirement for undergraduate programs), (2) passing the comprehensive exam, thesis/capstone, or practicum required by your program, (3) filing your application as a candidate for graduation through your Dean's Office and the Registrar within the announced window, (4) settling all clearances (library, dormitory if applicable, college, etc.), and (5) paying any graduation-related fees. Exact deadlines, fees, and forms vary per academic year and per campus — please verify with your Dean's Office and the Registrar through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

To graduate from CvSU you need to complete the units in your curriculum, finish program-specific exit requirements (thesis/capstone/practicum/comprehensive exam as applicable), complete NSTP for undergraduate programs, file a graduation application with your Dean and the Registrar in the announced window, and settle all clearances and fees. Deadlines and forms vary per year — please verify with your Dean's Office.

Para mag-graduate sa CvSU, kailangang kumpleto ang units sa inyong kurikulum (kasama ang NSTP), matapos ang thesis/capstone/practicum o comprehensive exam (depende sa programa), maghain ng aplikasyon bilang candidate for graduation sa Dean at Registrar, at ayusin ang lahat ng clearance at fees. Ang deadlines at forms ay nag-iiba bawat taon — kumpirmahin sa Dean's Office at sa Registrar.

At each campus, the Guidance Services Unit is headed by the Campus Guidance Coordinator. Services typically include academic, career, and personal counseling, plus orientation and information programs. For verified Guidance Office contact details at your campus, please refer to the campus subdomain (e.g., trece.cvsu.edu.ph/osas/guidance-and-counseling) or your campus OSAS page. For after-hours mental-health concerns, the Philippines' NCMH Crisis Line is reachable 24/7 toll-free at 1553 (landline).

For your campus's Guidance contact, see the campus subdomain. For after-hours crisis support, NCMH Crisis Line: 1553 (24/7 toll-free landline). health_services: CvSU operates a Health Service Unit (clinic) at major campuses, typically providing first aid, basic consultation, issuance of medical certificates for school-related purposes, and the pre-enrollment medical examination required for new students. Service hours, available staff (nurse on duty / visiting physician), and the specific services offered vary per campus. For verified Health Service Unit hours and contact details, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph or visit your campus's Student Services area. For medical emergencies, go to the nearest hospital or call 911.

Your campus Health Service Unit (clinic) provides basic care, first aid, and pre-enrollment medical clearance. Hours and staff differ per campus — verify with your campus's Student Services or https://cvsu.edu.ph. For emergencies, call 911 or go to the nearest hospital.

May Health Service Unit (klinika) ang CvSU sa mga pangunahing campus, na nag-aalok ng first aid, basic consultation, at medical certificate para sa school-related needs. Ang oras at available na staff ay nag-iiba bawat campus. Para sa medical emergency, tumawag ng 911 o pumunta sa pinakamalapit na ospital.
homeschool_admission: Homeschooled applicants are generally admitted to CvSU if they hold a recognized DepEd Senior High School completion certificate or its equivalent (e.g., through a DepEd-accredited homeschool provider, or through the ALS A&E pathway). You'll typically submit the equivalent of Form 138 (issued by your accredited provider or DepEd), and the standard freshman documents. The CvSU entrance examination still applies. For the current requirements specific to homeschooled applicants, please verify with the Admissions Office through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Homeschooled applicants are accepted at CvSU if their credentials are DepEd-recognized (accredited homeschool provider or ALS pathway). The standard entrance exam and freshman requirements still apply. Confirm details with Admissions.

Tumatanggap ang CvSU ng homeschooled applicants kung ang kanilang credentials ay kilala ng DepEd. Karaniwang ginagamit ang katumbas ng Form 138 mula sa accredited provider. Magtanong sa Admissions Office sa https://cvsu.edu.ph.

international_students:
CvSU INTERNATIONAL / FOREIGN STUDENT ADMISSIONS:

CvSU accepts foreign nationals subject to CHED and university regulations.

TYPICAL REQUIREMENTS:
- Accomplished application form
- Authenticated/apostilled academic credentials (HS diploma, transcripts)
- Passport copy (biodata page)
- Student Visa (9F) — typically processed AFTER admission
- English-proficiency proof (if from a non-English-speaking country)
- Medical/health clearance
- Police clearance (NBI or equivalent from home country)

TYPICAL PROCESS:
1. Submit application to the Office of Admissions (Main Campus, Indang)
2. Await credential evaluation and Notice of Admission (NOA)
3. Process the 9F Student Visa with the Bureau of Immigration
4. Complete enrollment procedures

TUITION NOTE:
Free tuition under RA 10931 may NOT apply to foreign students. Contact the Cashier's Office for the applicable fees.

FOR VERIFIED CONTACT DETAILS:
Office of Admissions — email: admissions@cvsu.edu.ph
Website: www.cvsu.edu.ph (use the official directory for current phone numbers).
intramurals_sportsfest: Intramurals (Sportsfest) at CvSU is the annual university or campus-level competition pitting colleges/departments against each other in sports, cheering, cultural performances, and academic events. Dates are set each academic year by Student Affairs and the Sports Office. Participation is typically free for enrolled students — try-outs and team selection are handled at the college/department level. For the current year's schedule and event list, follow CvSU's verified Facebook page and your campus Student Affairs office.
Intramurals at CvSU is an annual college-vs-college sports and culture festival. Dates set per year; team selection at the college/department level. Follow OSAS announcements for the schedule.
Ang Intramurals/Sportsfest sa CvSU ay taunang labanan ng mga college/department sa sports, cheer, at kultura. Itinatakda ang petsa bawat taon. Sumunod sa anunsiyo ng OSAS at Sports Office.
it_cs_courses: CvSU's College of Engineering and Information Technology (CEIT) offers computing programs at the Main Campus, including BS Computer Science and BS Information Technology, alongside the College's engineering programs. Computing programs are also offered at satellite campuses — per the official site, BS Computer Science at the Carmona Campus and BS Information Technology at the Cavite City Campus both earned AACCUP Level III re-accredited status in 2026. The official CEIT page is at https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ceit-academic-programs/. For availability at other campuses, verify via https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/campuses/.

CvSU's CEIT (College of Engineering and Information Technology) at the Main Campus offers BS Computer Science and BS Information Technology, along with engineering programs. Satellite campuses also offer computing: BS Computer Science at Carmona and BS Information Technology at Cavite City (both AACCUP Level III re-accredited, 2026). Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ceit-academic-programs/

Ang CEIT (College of Engineering and Information Technology) sa Main Campus ng CvSU ay nag-aalok ng BS Computer Science, BS Information Technology, at mga engineering programs. May computing programs din sa satellite campuses: BSCS sa Carmona at BSIT sa Cavite City (parehong AACCUP Level III re-accredited, 2026). Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ceit-academic-programs/
ladderized_education: Ladderized education in the Philippines is governed by RA 10647 (Ladderized Education Act of 2014), which allows TESDA-issued National Certificates (NC I, II, III) and CHED-recognized undergraduate units to ladder into each other under qualified programs. Some CvSU programs participate in ladderized arrangements, allowing students to exit with a certificate or AS/AAS credential and re-enter to complete a bachelor's degree later. Availability is program-specific — verify which CvSU programs are ladderized with the Office of Academic Affairs through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

RA 10647 enables ladderized education between TESDA NCs and CHED-aligned bachelor's programs. CvSU participates in select ladderized programs — verify availability with the Office of Academic Affairs.
Ang ladderized education (RA 10647) ay nagpapahintulot ng pagdaloy mula TESDA papunta sa bachelor's degree. May ilang programa sa CvSU na ladderized — kumpirmahin sa Office of Academic Affairs.
late_enrollment: Late enrollment may be allowed at CvSU within a brief grace window after the regular enrollment period closes, usually with a late-enrollment fee. Beyond that window, you typically need to file for a Leave of Absence for the semester and re-enroll in the next term. Specific late-enrollment dates, penalties, and limits vary per campus and per academic year. Please contact your campus Registrar urgently — late-enrollment grace periods are short. For current dates, see https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Late enrollment is usually permitted within a short window with a penalty fee. If you miss it entirely, you may need to file an LOA for the semester. Verify the exact grace dates with your campus Registrar.
Maaaring pwede ang late enrollment sa loob ng maikling palugit na may kaakibat na multa. Lampas doon, maaaring kailangan mong mag-file ng LOA. Makipag-ugnayan kaagad sa Registrar ng inyong campus.
lgbtq_support: CvSU is committed to a respectful and inclusive learning environment for all students, including LGBTQ+ students. Discrimination, harassment, or bullying based on Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity and Expression (SOGIE) is prohibited under the Safe Spaces Act (RA 11313) and the Anti-Bullying Act (RA 10627). If you experience or witness discrimination, you can report to the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), Guidance Office, or the campus Gender and Development (GAD) Office. For policies on preferred name, pronouns, and other gender-related student services, please coordinate with OSAS and the GAD Office at your campus.
CvSU prohibits SOGIE-based discrimination under the Safe Spaces Act and Anti-Bullying Act. Report concerns to OSAS, Guidance, or the campus GAD Office.
Ipinagbabawal ng CvSU ang diskriminasyon batay sa SOGIE alinsunod sa Safe Spaces Act at Anti-Bullying Act. Maaaring iulat ang anumang isyu sa OSAS, Guidance, o sa GAD Office ng inyong campus.
library: The Main Campus library is the LADISLAO N. DIWA MEMORIAL LIBRARY. Its mission, as stated officially, is to provide excellent, equitable and relevant library services and resources in support of the University's mission, and to support the University in its main thrust of fields of expertise by providing adequate, updated, and relevant collection of research and reference materials. Each CvSU campus also operates its own library. For the union catalog and campus library information across the CvSU library system, visit https://library.cvsu.edu.ph/.

CvSU's library at the Main Campus is the Ladislao N. Diwa Memorial Library, supporting research and reference needs. Each satellite campus also has its own library. For the integrated library system (collections, services, campus library pages), visit https://library.cvsu.edu.ph/.

Ang library sa Main Campus ay ang Ladislao N. Diwa Memorial Library, na nag-aalok ng excellent, equitable, at relevant na library services at resources. May sariling library ang bawat campus. Tingnan ang https://library.cvsu.edu.ph/ para sa kumpletong collection at services ng buong CvSU library system.

lms_online_classes: CvSU instructors deliver online and hybrid components of their courses through various platforms — commonly Google Classroom, Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and (where deployed) the university's learning-management system. Which platform you'll use depends on your instructor. Your CvSU email account is typically used to access these platforms. For platform-specific access issues or account problems, contact your instructor first, then ICT/MIS if it's an account/permissions issue.

Online/hybrid classes at CvSU use various platforms (Google Classroom, MS Teams, Zoom, the campus LMS). Your CvSU email logs you in. For access issues, ask your instructor; for account problems, ICT/MIS.
Ang mga online/hybrid class sa CvSU ay gumagamit ng iba't ibang platform (Google Classroom, MS Teams, Zoom, LMS). Ang CvSU email ay nagiging login. Magtanong sa instructor o ICT/MIS.
lost_and_found: If you lost an item on campus, check with Campus Security first — most CvSU campuses maintain a Lost & Found at the Security Office or OSAS. Describe the item, where and approximately when you lost it, and leave your contact details so they can reach you if it turns up. For stolen items, file a report with Campus Security; serious incidents may also be reported to the local PNP station. For lost IDs, see the ID replacement intent.
Lost items: check with Campus Security/OSAS — they maintain Lost & Found. For stolen items, file a report with Security; serious cases also go to local PNP. For lost IDs, request replacement.
Para sa nawalan: tanungin ang Campus Security/OSAS — sila ang may Lost & Found. Para sa ninakaw, mag-file ng report sa Security; sa malulubha, sa lokal na PNP.
lost_id_replacement: To replace a lost CvSU student ID, you'll typically need to: (1) execute a notarized Affidavit of Loss describing how the ID was lost, (2) secure the ID replacement form from the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) or the Registrar (process varies per campus), (3) submit the affidavit, your COR/registration form, and a recent ID photo, (4) pay the applicable replacement fee at the Cashier's Office, (5) claim your new ID per the schedule given. The replacement fee, exact office, and processing time vary by campus — please confirm with your campus OSAS/Registrar. For verified contact details, visit https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Lost-ID replacement typically requires a notarized Affidavit of Loss, a recent photo, your COR/registration form, and a replacement fee paid at the Cashier. The exact office handling this and the processing time vary per campus — please confirm with your campus OSAS or Registrar.

Para sa replacement ng nawalang CvSU ID: kailangan ng notarized Affidavit of Loss, bagong ID picture, COR, at replacement fee na ibabayad sa Cashier. Ang opisina na hahawak nito at ang processing time ay nag-iiba bawat campus — kumpirmahin sa OSAS o Registrar ng inyong campus.

nstp:
NATIONAL SERVICE TRAINING PROGRAM (NSTP) AT CvSU:

NSTP is MANDATORY for all first-year college students — 2 semesters (NSTP 1 & NSTP 2).

THREE COMPONENTS (choose one):
- ROTC: Reserve Officers' Training Corps — military/AFP training
- CWTS: Civic Welfare Training Service — community development projects
- LTS: Literacy Training Service — teaching literacy in communities

ENROLLMENT:
- Register during first-year enrollment
- Coordinate with your college/department for available components
- Some campuses may offer limited components — check with OSAS

COMPLETION:
- NSTP certificate is REQUIRED for graduation
- Must complete both NSTP 1 and NSTP 2

CONTACT: Office of Student Affairs & Services (OSAS) or your department adviser.

Ang NSTP (National Service Training Program) ay sapilitan para sa lahat ng unang taon na estudyante sa CvSU. Pumili ng isa sa tatlong component: ROTC, CWTS, o LTS. Dapat makumpleto ang NSTP 1 at NSTP 2 para sa sertipiko na kailangan sa graduation. Makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS o sa iyong departamento para sa detalye.
nursing_health_programs: CvSU's College of Nursing offers BS Nursing, BS Medical Technology, and Diploma in Midwifery (per the official Colleges listing). BS Medical Technology holds AACCUP Level III re-accredited status (2026), and CvSU posted a 97.33% overall performance rating (73 of 75 examinees) in the March 2026 Medical Technologists Licensure Examination — well above the 84.17% national passing rate. Availability and slots may vary per campus and academic year. For the current admission and program details, see https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/ and the College of Nursing's official page.

The College of Nursing at CvSU offers BS Nursing, BS Medical Technology (AACCUP Level III re-accredited, 2026), and Diploma in Midwifery. CvSU scored 97.33% in the March 2026 Medical Technologists Licensure Exam (national: 84.17%). See https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/ for the latest details.

Ang College of Nursing ng CvSU ay nag-aalok ng BS Nursing, BS Medical Technology (AACCUP Level III re-accredited, 2026), at Diploma in Midwifery. Nakakuha ang CvSU ng 97.33% sa March 2026 Medical Technologists Licensure Exam (pambansa: 84.17%). Para sa kasalukuyang detalye, tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.
off_campus_activities:
OFF-CAMPUS ACTIVITIES — Student Organizations (OSAS):

WHO MAY AVAIL: Recognized student organizations.

REQUIREMENTS (before activity):
- Request letter from the org president
- Activity proposal
- Invitation letter from the organizer
- CHED Memorandum + list of CHED requirements (from SDS Personnel)
- Curriculum, destination, handbook/manual
- Notarized parent's permit
- Medical clearance (Health Services)
- Personnel-in-charge IDs
- First aid kit, fees, mobility plan, insurance
- Certificate of compliance (from SDS)

REQUIREMENTS (after activity):
- Learning journals
- Assessment / evaluation report
- Expenditure report

PROCESS:
1. Submit request letter to SDS for initial evaluation (or via email osasmain.studentdevelopment@cvsu.edu.ph).
2. Receive Notice of Completion of Requirements.
3. SDS signs and endorses to OSAS Dean → Vice President for Academic Affairs for approval.
4. Approved letter is released to the organization.

FEES: None.
Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — OSAS, p.540.

Off-campus activity approval (student orgs): isumite ang request letter, activity proposal, invitation, CHED memo, parent's permit, medical clearance, atbp. sa SDS sa OSAS. Pagkatapos ng activity, magsumite ng learning journal, assessment report, at expenditure report. Libre. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.540)
off_topic_homework: I'm focused on helping you with CvSU information rather than doing your coursework. Submitting work that isn't your own may violate CvSU's academic integrity policy. I'd be happy to point you to CvSU resources that can help you do the work yourself — for example, your campus library, tutoring services through the Guidance Office, or your subject teacher's consultation hours. Is there a CvSU service I can help you find?

I won't do your coursework — it goes against CvSU's academic integrity policy. I can point you to CvSU resources that help you do it yourself: library, tutoring services through Guidance, or your teacher's consultation hours.
Hindi po ako tutulong sa inyong assignment dahil labag ito sa academic integrity policy ng CvSU. Maaari ko kayong ituro sa library, tutoring service ng Guidance, o consultation hours ng inyong guro.
ojt_internship: OJT / Internship at CvSU is coordinated by your college's OJT coordinator in partnership with the Career and Placement Office under OSAS. Required hours, eligible partner companies, and documentation (MOA, endorsement letter, daily time record, internship report) depend on your specific program and college. Watch for the OJT orientation announced by your department, then coordinate placement with your coordinator. For the current OJT partners and process at your campus, see your department and https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/.

OJT is coordinated by your college's OJT coordinator with OSAS's Career and Placement. Specifics (hours, partners, paperwork) vary by program. Attend the department orientation; coordinate placement with your coordinator. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/

Ang OJT sa CvSU ay sa pamamagitan ng OJT coordinator ng college at Career and Placement (nasa OSAS). Ang hours, partners, at paperwork ay nag-iiba bawat programa. Dumalo sa department orientation. Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/
online_programs:
CvSU ONLINE & FLEXIBLE LEARNING PROGRAMS:

ETEEAP (Expanded Tertiary Education Equivalency and Accreditation Program):
- For working professionals seeking a college degree through prior learning recognition
- Credits work experience, training, and non-formal education toward a degree
- Available for select programs — inquire at the Office of Academic Affairs
- Requirements: Work portfolio, assessment/evaluation, applicable fees

FLEXIBLE / BLENDED LEARNING:
- Some programs offer hybrid delivery (face-to-face + online components)
- Availability varies per campus and program per semester
- Requires internet access and compatible device

MODULAR DISTANCE LEARNING (MDL):

- Available for select programs as approved by CHED

HOW TO APPLY FOR ETEEAP:
