2. Submit your educational & professional portfolio
3. Undergo assessment and evaluation
4. Enroll if qualified

CONTACT:
Office of Academic Affairs | Email: academic@cvsu.edu.ph
Website: www.cvsu.edu.ph

Oo, may flexible learning programs ang CvSU! Ang ETEEAP ay para sa mga nagtatrabaho na gustong makakuha ng degree batay sa kanilang karanasan. May iba ring programs na nag-aalok ng blended o modular learning. Para sa ETEEAP, makipag-ugnayan sa Office of Academic Affairs sa Main Campus. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
out_of_scope: That question is outside my scope — I'm focused on CvSU-related information. I can help with admissions, enrollment, programs, tuition, scholarships, campus services, and similar university topics. Is there something CvSU-related I can help you with instead?
I specialize in CvSU information, so I can't really help with that. Is there a CvSU-related question — about admissions, programs, fees, scholarships, or campus services — I can answer for you?

Hindi po sakop ng aking tulong ang katanungang iyon — nakatuon po ako sa impormasyon tungkol sa CvSU. May maitutulong po ba ako sa inyo tungkol sa admissions, programa, bayarin, scholarship, o serbisyo ng campus?
parking_oncampus: Parking on CvSU campuses is typically allowed for students, faculty, and staff in designated lots — usually with a campus parking sticker/pass issued by Security or the Administrative Services office. Motorcycle and car parking areas are separate. Rules, sticker fees, and pass procedures vary per campus. For the current parking-pass procedure at your campus, please contact your campus Security/Administrative office.
Parking on CvSU campuses uses designated lots with a parking sticker/pass issued by Security or Admin Services. Rules and fees vary per campus.
May designated parking sa mga CvSU campus, na may parking sticker/pass mula sa Security o Admin Services. Iba-iba ang patakaran at bayad bawat campus.
prerequisites: Many CvSU subjects have prerequisites — subjects you must pass first before enrolling in the next one. The prerequisite list is in your curriculum and the course catalog of your program. In most cases, taking a subject without its prerequisite is not allowed; petitions or waivers may be considered by the Dean only in special circumstances. Co-requisites are subjects that must be taken together (or one must be taken first). Always check prerequisites before enrolling — the Registrar's system normally enforces them.
Prerequisites are enforced — you generally can't enroll in a subject without having passed its prereq. Waivers are rare and require Dean approval. Check the curriculum or ask your adviser.
May mga subject sa CvSU na may prerequisite — kailangan munang pumasa sa isa bago kumuha ng kasunod. Tingnan ang inyong kurikulum o magtanong sa adviser.
printing_photocopy: Printing, photocopying, scanning, and binding services are typically available at the campus library and at private shops just outside campus gates — these are operated by third parties, so rates and turnaround vary. For thesis printing and binding, allow extra time around graduation rush. There is no university-wide standard rate that I can verify.
Printing/photocopy/scan/binding services are usually available at the library and outside-gate shops. Rates and turnaround vary by provider — especially during graduation rush.
Karaniwang may printing, photocopy, scan, at binding sa library at sa labas ng gate ng CvSU. Ang presyo at oras ng paghahanda ay nag-iiba bawat tindahan.
pwd_accessibility: CvSU follows national laws on access for persons with disabilities, including the Magna Carta for Persons with Disabilities (RA 7277, as amended). Students with disabilities are entitled to reasonable accommodations for academic activities and assessments. Common services include ramp/lift access (where infrastructure permits), priority seating, exam accommodations (extended time, scribe, accessible format), and coordination with the Guidance/OSAS office. To request accommodations, contact the OSAS / Guidance Office and your College Dean as early as possible — preferably at enrollment.
PWD students at CvSU are entitled to reasonable accommodations under RA 7277. Request via OSAS/Guidance and your Dean as early as possible (ideally at enrollment).
Ang mga PWD students ay may karapatan sa reasonable accommodations sa CvSU sa ilalim ng RA 7277. Magrequest sa OSAS/Guidance at sa Dean nang maaga, idealmente sa enrollment.
registrar: The Office of the University Registrar (OUR) at CvSU handles your academic records, including Transcript of Records (TOR), Certificate of Enrollment, Certificate of Registration (COR), Diploma, Good Moral, Honorable Dismissal, and authentication of records. Each campus has its own Registrar office. For the verified Registrar contact details at your campus, please refer to the Citizens Charter and campus directory at https://cvsu.edu.ph. Document processes (forms, fees, timelines) are documented in the CvSU Citizens Charter. See https://cvsu.edu.ph (search 'Citizens Charter') for the latest edition.

The Office of the University Registrar handles academic records — TOR, COR, Diploma, Good Moral, Honorable Dismissal, authentication. Each campus has its own Registrar. Document processes and fees are in the CvSU Citizens Charter on https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Ang Office of the University Registrar ang nag-aasikaso ng academic records — TOR, COR, Diploma, Good Moral, Honorable Dismissal, at authentication. May sariling Registrar ang bawat campus. Ang detalye ng proseso at fees ay nasa CvSU Citizens Charter sa https://cvsu.edu.ph.
religious_orgs: CvSU has student organizations across various faith traditions — typically including Catholic groups, evangelical/Christian fellowships, and others, depending on campus. The Main Campus and some others have a chapel; prayer rooms may be available at other campuses. Mass and worship schedules are set by the resident chaplain or partner ministries and posted at the chapel. For the list of recognized religious organizations at your campus, contact OSAS.
Faith-based student organizations are present at CvSU (Catholic groups, Christian fellowships, and others, depending on campus). For schedules and the recognized-org list, contact your campus OSAS.
May iba't ibang faith-based student organizations sa CvSU. Para sa iskedyul ng misa o worship at listahan ng recognized religious orgs, makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS.
removal_exam: A removal examination (commonly called 'removal') may be offered at the end of the semester to students with a conditional grade or who failed a specific subject under defined circumstances — but availability is at the discretion of the instructor and the department, and not all subjects permit one. The procedure and fee are usually set per department. If a removal is not available or you fail it, the standard path is to retake the subject in a future semester. Confirm with your subject teacher and Department Chair.

Removal exams may be allowed at end-of-semester for conditional/failed grades — availability varies per subject and instructor. If unavailable or if you fail it, retake the subject. Confirm with your Department Chair.
Ang removal exam ay maaaring iaalok sa katapusan ng semester para sa conditional/failed grade — ngunit hindi lahat ng subject ay may removal. Magtanong sa instructor at Department Chair.
remove_inc_grade: An INC (Incomplete) grade at CvSU means the subject's requirements are not yet fully met. To remove it, you must complete the deficient requirement (project, exam, paper) with your subject teacher within the prescribed period — usually within one academic year. Once the teacher submits the completion to the Registrar, your INC is replaced with the final grade. If you fail to complete within the prescribed period, the INC typically converts to 5.0 (Failed). Coordinate directly with the subject teacher and verify deadlines with the Registrar.

INC means an unfulfilled requirement. Complete it with the teacher within the prescribed period (usually one year), and the teacher submits the corrected grade to the Registrar. Unresolved INCs may become 5.0.
Ang INC ay kulang na requirement. Tapusin ito sa loob ng prescribed period (kadalasang isang taon) at i-submit ng instructor sa Registrar ang final grade. Kung hindi natapos, maaaring maging 5.0.
research_ethics: Research involving human or animal subjects at CvSU is reviewed by the appropriate research ethics committee (IRB or its equivalent) to ensure compliance with national rules (PHREB / DOST-PCHRD guidelines, the Data Privacy Act of 2012, the Animal Welfare Act, etc.). Typical requirements include: research proposal, data privacy plan, informed consent forms, and adviser endorsement. Submit through your College Research Office or the University Research Office. For the current ethics clearance process and forms, please contact the research office at your campus.

Human/animal subjects research at CvSU goes through ethics review (IRB or equivalent), with national rules from PHREB, the Data Privacy Act, and Animal Welfare Act applying. Coordinate through your College Research Office.
Ang research na may human o animal subjects sa CvSU ay sumasailalim sa ethics review batay sa PHREB, Data Privacy Act, at Animal Welfare Act. Mag-coordinate sa College Research Office.
retention_policy: CvSU's academic retention standards are governed by the University Student Handbook and the applicable CHED Memorandum Orders for each program. In general, students must maintain a satisfactory General Weighted Average (GWA) and must not accumulate more than the allowed number of failing grades per semester. Students who fall below the required academic standing may be placed on academic probation, given a load restriction, or, in severe cases, recommended for dismissal from the program. Specific GWA cutoffs and the number of allowable failures differ by college and program. I strongly recommend consulting your College's Student Handbook or the Registrar for your program's exact retention rules.

Academic retention at CvSU follows the University Student Handbook provisions on Scholastic Standing. Key points: (1) INC (Incomplete) grades must typically be completed within the prescribed period — unresolved INCs may convert to failing marks; (2) students on academic probation often carry a reduced study load; (3) dismissal from a program may result from repeated failures beyond the threshold set by your college. Maximum residency rules (how many years you may take to finish a degree) also apply per CHED guidelines. Please verify the exact figures with your Registrar or academic adviser, as rules vary by program and year level.

If you're concerned about your academic standing, the first step is to visit your Campus Registrar or your program's academic adviser — they can tell you your current GWA, what the retention cutoff is for your specific program, and whether you are at risk of academic probation or dismissal. CvSU also has an appeals process for students facing academic disqualification; this is handled through the Office of the Registrar and the Dean's Office. Would you like guidance on which office to contact, or do you have a more specific question about grades or program policies?
retention_policy_appeal: If you believe a grade was recorded incorrectly or unfairly, you may file a formal grade reconsideration request. The process typically starts with your subject teacher/professor, then escalates to the Department Chair, then the College Dean, and finally to the Registrar if unresolved. Timelines for filing are strict — usually within one semester of the grade being posted — so act quickly. You will likely need to submit a written appeal, any supporting evidence (attendance records, submitted work), and the endorsement of your adviser. Consult your campus Registrar for the exact procedure and deadlines.

For academic dismissal or disqualification, CvSU provides an appeal or petition for retention process through the Office of the Registrar and the Dean's Office of your college. You would typically submit a formal petition letter, your academic records, and supporting documents (medical certificates, financial hardship affidavits, etc. if applicable). The case may be reviewed by an Academic Standing Committee. Some campuses also allow students to shift to a different program rather than face outright dismissal. The Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) can also provide academic counseling and guidance on your options. Please verify current timelines with your campus Registrar.

Options after academic disqualification at CvSU may include: (1) filing a petition for retention with the Dean's Office, (2) shifting to a different program within CvSU where your academic standing qualifies you, (3) applying for re-admission after a required gap period, or (4) transferring to another institution. Each path has specific requirements and deadlines. The Guidance and Counseling Office at your campus can help you understand your standing and plan next steps. Do not wait — timelines for appeals are usually strictly enforced. Would you like to know more about a specific option?
retention_policy_grades: CvSU uses a numerical grading scale where 1.0 is the highest (Excellent/Outstanding) and 5.0 is Failing. The common equivalents are: 1.0 = 97–100%, 1.25 = 94–96%, 1.50 = 91–93%, 1.75 = 88–90%, 2.0 = 85–87%, 2.25 = 82–84%, 2.50 = 79–81%, 2.75 = 76–78%, 3.0 = 75% (Passing, minimum), 4.0 = Conditional/Incomplete (INC), 5.0 = Failed. A grade of 3.0 is the minimum passing mark. INC grades must be resolved within the prescribed period; unresolved INCs may become 5.0. Please confirm the exact equivalency table with your Registrar since some programs may use slight variations.

Your General Weighted Average (GWA) at CvSU is computed by multiplying each subject's grade by its unit weight, summing those products, then dividing by total units enrolled. A lower numerical GWA (closer to 1.0) is better. For Latin honors at graduation: Summa Cum Laude typically requires a GWA of 1.20 or better with no failing or dropped subjects; Magna Cum Laude around 1.21–1.45; Cum Laude around 1.46–1.75 — but these thresholds should be verified with the Registrar as they are governed by the Student Handbook and may be updated. Failing subjects (5.0) significantly pull your GWA up (worse) and may trigger academic probation.

The key grades to know at CvSU: 3.0 is the minimum passing grade (equivalent to 75%), and 5.0 is failing. An INC (Incomplete) grade of 4.0 means you have unfinished requirements — it is not failing yet but must be resolved within the period stated in the Student Handbook, or it converts to 5.0. A DRP (Dropped) grade is recorded if you officially withdrew from a subject before the deadline; this differs from being informally absent. Your GWA is affected by all grades and units. For graduation honors or dean's list eligibility, check the exact GWA cutoffs in your campus's Student Handbook or ask the Registrar.
returning_student: Returning students (those who took a Leave of Absence, dropped out for a semester, or were AWOL) typically need to apply for re-admission through the Registrar and their college Dean's Office. Required documents usually include: written letter/application for re-admission, your most recent COR or grade record, clearance documents, and any medical/personal documentation that supported your absence (if applicable). Eligibility depends on how long you've been away and your academic standing when you left. Maximum residency rules also apply. For the verified process at your campus, please contact the Registrar through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

To return to CvSU after an absence, file a re-admission request with your Dean and the Registrar. Required documents typically include a letter of intent, your prior records, and clearance. Eligibility and timelines depend on duration of absence and standing.

Para bumalik sa CvSU pagkatapos ng pagkawala (LOA, AWOL, drop-out), kailangan ng aplikasyon ng re-admission sa Dean at Registrar. Karaniwang kailangan ng letter of intent at dating records. Ang eligibility ay depende sa haba ng pagkakaroon.
scholarship:
CvSU offers several scholarship and financial-assistance programs, including (per the official Office of Student Affairs and Services and Citizens Charter):
- CvSU State Scholarship
- Job Experience Program
- RA 7160 scholarship
- Entrance Scholarships for Valedictorian, Salutatorian, and 1st/2nd/3rd Honorable Mention high-school graduates
- Government scholarships (e.g., DOST-SEI, CHED) and LGU scholarships availed through CvSU
- Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) and free tuition under RA 10931 for qualified undergraduate Filipino students

Application is managed by the Financial Assistance Services Unit and distributed through OSAS. You'll typically need an accomplished scholarship form and supporting documents such as your parents' joint income tax return or a BIR Affidavit of Non-Filing.

For current eligibility, deadlines, and the specific scholarships open at your campus, visit OSAS or https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/

Mga scholarship programs ng CvSU: CvSU State Scholarship, Job Experience Program, RA 7160 scholarship, Entrance Scholarships (Valedictorian, Salutatorian, 1st/2nd/3rd Honorable Mention), government scholarships (DOST-SEI, CHED), LGU scholarships, TES, at libreng tuition sa ilalim ng RA 10931. Ang Financial Assistance Services Unit at OSAS ang nag-asikaso ng aplikasyon. Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/
second_courser: Yes, CvSU generally accepts second-coursers (applicants who already hold an undergraduate degree). You'll typically submit your previous TOR, diploma copy, Honorable Dismissal, and the standard application documents. Note: RA 10931 (free tuition) usually does NOT cover students who already earned an undergraduate degree, so a second course is likely subject to applicable tuition and fees. For the current procedure, eligible programs, and fee schedule, please verify with the Admissions Office and Cashier's Office through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Second-coursers can apply to CvSU with their previous TOR, diploma, and Honorable Dismissal. RA 10931 may not cover a second undergraduate degree, so tuition may apply — verify with the Cashier's Office.

Tumatanggap ang CvSU ng second-courser. Kailangan ng dating TOR, diploma, at Honorable Dismissal. Tandaan: maaaring hindi sakop ng RA 10931 ang second degree — magtanong sa Cashier's Office sa https://cvsu.edu.ph.
senior_high_to_college: CvSU generally welcomes graduates of any Senior High School (SHS) strand for admission, but the strand you finished can influence how well-prepared you are for a specific program and, in some cases, whether you need bridging subjects. For example, STEM aligns naturally with engineering, IT, computer science, and the health sciences; ABM aligns with business and accountancy; HUMSS aligns with education, social sciences, and communication; TVL with technology and trade programs. You can still cross-apply from any strand — academic readiness is assessed via the entrance exam and the receiving college's evaluation. Specific bridging requirements vary per program.

Any SHS strand can apply to CvSU programs, but alignment may simplify your transition. STEM → engineering/IT/health, ABM → business, HUMSS → education/social sciences/communication, TVL → technology/trades. Cross-strand applicants may need bridging subjects depending on the program.

Tumatanggap ang CvSU ng kahit anong SHS strand para sa admission. Maaaring magkaroon ng bridging subjects depende sa programa. Ang STEM ay angkop para sa engineering/IT, ABM sa business, HUMSS sa edukasyon/social sciences, at TVL sa technology/trade.
shifting_program: To shift programs within CvSU: (1) secure the shifting form from the Registrar / your current Dean, (2) get endorsement from your current Dean, (3) apply to the receiving college (your target program may require interview, minimum GWA, or available slots), (4) once approved, the Registrar processes the shift and credit evaluation. Shifting is normally done before or during the enrollment period. For the current shifting window and any program-specific cutoffs (some competitive programs have stricter requirements), please coordinate with your current and target Dean's Offices.

Program shifting at CvSU requires endorsement from your current Dean and acceptance by the receiving college. Submit the shifting form, your grades, and any required personal statement to the Registrar during the open shifting period. Exact rules differ by program — verify with both Dean's offices.

Para mag-shift ng programa sa CvSU: kumuha ng shifting form sa Registrar/Dean, kumuha ng endorsement mula sa kasalukuyang Dean, mag-apply sa target na college, at hintayin ang approval. Ang competitive programs ay may stricter requirements. Kumpirmahin sa parehong Dean's office.
special_class: A special class (sometimes called a 'tutorial', 'petition class', or 'independent study') may be allowed at CvSU when a subject is not offered in the current semester but a small number of students need it (often graduating students or those affected by curriculum changes). The procedure typically requires: a petition signed by the affected students (a minimum number applies), a willing faculty member, approval of the Department Chair and the College Dean, and any applicable fees. Approval is not guaranteed. Coordinate with your Department Chair early.

Special/petition classes are allowed when a subject isn't offered but several students need it. Requires a faculty member willing to teach, a minimum number of petitioners, and Department/Dean approval.

Maaaring magkaroon ng special/petition class kung hindi inaalok ang isang subject. Kailangan ng faculty na handa, minimum na bilang ng nag-pe-petition, at pag-apruba ng Department at Dean. Magtanong sa Department Chair.
sports_athletics: CvSU has varsity teams that compete in inter-collegiate and inter-university tournaments, including the State Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (SCUAA) Region IV-A and national qualifiers. Common varsity sports include basketball, volleyball, football, athletics (track & field), and others. Try-outs are typically held before each season and announced through the Sports Director's Office and Student Affairs. Athletic scholarships and stipends may be available for selected varsity athletes — terms vary. Check with your campus Sports Office for the current try-out schedule.

CvSU varsity teams compete in SCUAA and other tournaments. Try-outs happen before each season — check with your campus Sports Office for the current schedule. Athletic scholarships may be available for select varsity athletes.

May mga varsity team ang CvSU na sumasali sa SCUAA at iba pang tournament. May try-outs bago bawat season — magtanong sa Sports Office ng inyong campus. May athletic scholarship sa ilang varsity athletes.
student_clearance_signing:
STUDENT CLEARANCE — Accounting Office signing:

1. Get the Student Clearance Form from the College Registrar.
2. Present it at the Accounting Office.
3. Staff checks Student Account Assessment System for any balance.
4. If clear, the form is signed and returned to you.

FEES: None.
PROCESSING TIME: ~5 minutes.
Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — Accounting Office, p.147.

Para mag-pa-sign ng clearance sa Accounting: kunin muna ang Student Clearance Form sa College Registrar, dalhin sa Accounting Office, i-check nila kung may balance ka. Kung wala, papipirmahan agad. Libre at 5 minutes lang. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.147)
student_organizations: Student organizations at CvSU are recognized and coordinated by the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS). OSAS handles student development services, including student organization and socio-cultural affairs, student publication, and placement of students and graduates. To join, watch for the organization fair / open recruitment posted by OSAS or the org itself each academic year. For the recognized-org list at your campus, contact your campus OSAS via https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/.

OSAS oversees CvSU's recognized student organizations, including socio-cultural affairs, student publications, and student government. To join, watch for OSAS's organization recruitment or fair. For the recognized-org list, contact your campus OSAS. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/

Ang OSAS ang nangangasiwa ng mga recognized student organizations sa CvSU — kasama ang socio-cultural orgs, student publication, at student government. Para sumali, hintayin ang org recruitment ng OSAS o ng org mismo. Para sa listahan, makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS ng inyong campus. Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/
student_portal: CvSU has an official Student Portal at https://myportal.cvsu.edu.ph/. Your account credentials are issued by the campus ICT/MIS Office during enrollment. Through the portal you can typically view grades, manage online enrollment, and check your Certificate of Registration. For login issues or password resets, contact the ICT/MIS Office at your campus. Some campuses (e.g., Cavite City) also operate campus-specific student portals — verify with your campus.

Access the CvSU Student Portal at https://myportal.cvsu.edu.ph/ with the credentials issued by your campus ICT/MIS Office. For login problems or password resets, contact ICT/MIS. Some campuses operate campus-specific portals — verify with your campus.

Ang CvSU Student Portal ay nasa https://myportal.cvsu.edu.ph/. Ang account ay galing sa campus ICT/MIS Office sa enrollment. Para sa login problems o password reset, makipag-ugnayan sa ICT/MIS. May sariling portal ang ilang campus.
student_publication: CvSU campuses operate official student publications (the Main Campus's paper is The Gazette, with other campuses having their own publications). They typically recruit through writing tryouts/screening tests each academic year for staff writers, photojournalists, cartoonists, layout artists, and copy editors. Membership rules and recruitment timing vary per publication. To join, attend the publication's open call announcement (usually early in the semester) or visit their office at your campus.
Student publications at CvSU (e.g., The Gazette at Main Campus) recruit through writing tryouts each year. Watch for the open-call announcement or visit the publication office at your campus.
Ang student publications ng CvSU (tulad ng The Gazette sa Main Campus) ay may taunang tryouts. Sumunod sa open-call announcement o pumunta sa publication office ng inyong campus.
student_refund:
STUDENT REFUND (Accounting Office):

1. Get the Application for Refund Form from the Accounting Office.
2. Fill out and submit with required supporting documents (varies by nature of refund).
3. Accounting verifies completeness and stamps received.
4. Refund payroll is prepared after the cut-off date of the allowable refund period.

WHO MAY AVAIL: All students.
FEES: None.
PROCESSING TIME: ~2 days and 22 minutes total.
Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — Accounting Office, p.148.

Para mag-apply ng refund: kunin ang Application for Refund Form sa Accounting Office, sagutan at isumite kasama ng requirements. I-pa-verify, at maghihintay ng cutoff date para sa refund payroll. Walang bayad. Karaniwang umaabot ng 2 days at 22 minutes ang buong proseso. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.148)
subject_load_limits: The normal study load at CvSU is the load prescribed in your curriculum for the semester (typically 18–24 units depending on program and year level). Overload (carrying more units than prescribed) usually requires Dean's approval and is most commonly granted to graduating students or those with very strong academic standing. Underload (fewer units than prescribed) may be allowed with adviser approval if you're working, have health concerns, or are catching up on grades — but it may extend your time to graduate. Specific load limits vary per program; consult your adviser and the College Dean.

Normal load is what's prescribed in your curriculum. Overload requires Dean's approval (usually for graduating or top-performing students); underload requires adviser approval. Verify the exact limits with your adviser.

Ang normal na load sa CvSU ay ang nakatakda sa kurikulum (kadalasang 18–24 units). Ang overload ay nangangailangan ng pag-apruba ng Dean. Ang underload ay maaari ngunit posibleng makaapekto sa oras ng graduation. Magtanong sa adviser.
summer_class: A Summer Term is offered by CvSU when there is sufficient demand for a subject and faculty availability — typically used to retake failed subjects, advance in a curriculum, or catch up after an LOA. Summer offerings vary per campus and per program; not all subjects are offered every summer. Summer enrollment usually opens after the second semester ends. Note: tuition coverage under RA 10931 generally applies to summer for qualified undergraduate students, but verify with the Cashier's Office. Check https://cvsu.edu.ph for current summer offerings.

Summer Term offerings at CvSU vary per campus and per program. Used commonly for retakes or to advance. Enrollment opens after the second semester. For current summer offerings, check with your Dean's Office or https://cvsu.edu.ph.
Mayroong Summer Term sa CvSU kapag may sapat na demand at faculty availability. Karaniwang ginagamit ito para sa retake o pagkadami ng units. Ang summer offerings ay nag-iiba bawat campus.
supreme_student_government: Student government at CvSU is organized through Central Student Governments (CSG) at the campuses, federated university-wide as the Federation of Central Student Government. Students are also represented in the CvSU Board of Regents through the Student Regent (assisted by a Deputy Student Regent). Elections are typically held once each academic year with oversight by the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS). To run, you generally need to be a bona fide enrolled student and meet the academic and conduct requirements set by the election code. For this year's election dates and the constitution/by-laws, check with OSAS at your campus. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph

CvSU's student government is the Central Student Government (CSG) at each campus, federated as the Federation of Central Student Government, with a Student Regent (and Deputy Student Regent) representing students in the Board of Regents. Annual elections are overseen by OSAS — check OSAS for this year's election dates and eligibility rules.

Ang student government ng CvSU ay ang Central Student Government (CSG) sa bawat campus, na pinagsasama-sama bilang Federation of Central Student Government. May Student Regent (at Deputy Student Regent) na kumakatawan sa mga estudyante sa Board of Regents. May taunang halalan na pinangangasiwaan ng OSAS. Sumangguni sa OSAS para sa eligibility, deadlines, at petsa ng halalan.
tcp_admission:
TEACHER CERTIFICATE PROGRAM (TCP) ADMISSION:

WHO MAY AVAIL: Bachelor's degree graduates who want to earn units in Education (e.g. to qualify for the LET).

REQUIREMENTS:
- Accomplished Application Form for Admission (download at www.cvsu.edu.ph)
- Evaluation sheet signed by the Dean, College of Education
- 1 pc 1x1 picture (white background)
- 1 short brown envelope

PROCESS:
1. Submit documents to OSAS (online option may be announced).
2. OSAS forwards to College of Education for evaluation.
3. Receive Notice of Admission (NOA).
4. Present NOA to University Health Services Unit for medical examination.

FEES: None for application itself.
PROCESSING TIME: ~12 minutes (multi-stage).
Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — OSAS, p.538.

TCP (Teacher Certificate Program) — para sa mga may bachelor's degree na gustong kumuha ng education units. Magsumite sa OSAS ng application form, evaluation sheet mula sa Dean ng College of Education, 1x1 picture, at short brown envelope. Aabangan ang Notice of Admission, tapos mag-medical exam. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.538)

thesis_capstone: Most CvSU undergraduate programs require a thesis, capstone project, or culminating activity (the form varies by program — e.g., research thesis in science programs, capstone software project for IT/CS, business plan for entrepreneurship). The general flow: form a group (if required), choose a topic and adviser, defend a proposal, conduct the study/build the project, defend the final manuscript before a panel, revise as required, and submit the bound copy to the library/department. The exact format manual, deadlines, and committee composition vary per college; consult your department's thesis/capstone handbook.

Most CvSU programs require a thesis or capstone project. The flow: topic + adviser → proposal defense → execution → final defense → revisions → submission. Exact format and timelines vary per department.

Karamihan ng CvSU undergraduate programs ay may thesis o capstone. Ang flow: topic + adviser → proposal defense → ehekusyon → final defense → revisions → submission. Ang format at timelines ay depende sa departamento.
transcript_request_details: To request a Transcript of Records (TOR) from CvSU: (1) file the request at the Office of the University Registrar (online or in person, depending on what your campus supports), (2) pay the applicable fee at the Cashier, (3) attach any clearance required, (4) claim the document or have it mailed if that option is available. Common uses (board exam, employment, transfer, embassy) each may require a different number of copies and additional authentication (CAV for CHED, DFA apostille for foreign use, red ribbon for older documents). Processing time and fees vary — verify with the Registrar at your campus.

TOR request: file at the Registrar, pay at the Cashier, attach clearance, claim or have it mailed. Different uses (board, work, transfer, embassy) may need different authentication (CAV, DFA apostille). Verify fees and processing time with the Registrar.

Para sa TOR request: mag-file sa Registrar, magbayad sa Cashier, isumite ang clearance, at kunin o ipadala. Iba't ibang gamit (board exam, trabaho, transfer, embahada) ay maaaring kailangan ng iba't ibang authentication. Magtanong sa Registrar.
transferee_admission: Transferee applicants to CvSU typically need a Transcript of Records (TOR) and Certificate of Honorable Dismissal from the previous school, plus the same documents required of freshmen (PSA birth certificate, Good Moral, 2x2 photos, application form). After admission, your previous subjects undergo credit evaluation by the receiving college — some may credit toward your CvSU curriculum, others may not. Acceptance depends on slot availability per program and your previous academic standing. For the current transferee window, requirements, and exact contact, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Transferees need TOR, Honorable Dismissal, and the standard freshman documents. Credit evaluation is done by the receiving college after admission. Slots and exact rules vary per program — verify with the Admissions Office through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Ang mga transferee sa CvSU ay kailangan ng TOR at Honorable Dismissal mula sa dating paaralan, kasama ang standard na requirements ng freshman. Ang credit evaluation ay gagawin ng tatanggap na college pagkatapos ng admission. Para sa kasalukuyang window at requirements, bisitahin ang https://cvsu.edu.ph.
transport_to_campus: Public transport routes to CvSU vary by destination campus. For the Main Campus in Indang, common options include jeepneys/UV vans from nearby town terminals (e.g., from Dasmariñas, Trece Martires, or Tagaytay) into Indang town proper, then a tricycle to the campus gate. Other CvSU campuses have local terminals served by jeepneys and buses. Travel time and fare depend on traffic and route. For up-to-date local route information, ask current CvSU students at your target campus or check current commuting forums — bus/jeepney route schedules change.

Public transport to the Main Campus (Indang) typically goes via jeepneys/UV vans from Dasmariñas, Trece Martires, or Tagaytay, then tricycle to the gate. For other campuses, local terminals serve them. Route schedules change — verify locally.

Para sa Main Campus sa Indang, karaniwang sumasakay ng jeep/UV mula Dasmariñas, Trece, o Tagaytay, tapos tricycle papasok sa campus. Para sa ibang campus, may lokal na terminals. Magbago ang ruta — tanungin nang lokal.
tuition_fees:
CvSU TUITION FEES & FREE EDUCATION:

CvSU is covered by RA 10931 (Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act of 2017).

FREE TUITION COVERAGE (For qualified Filipino undergraduate students at SUCs):
- No tuition fee
- Other school fees covered as defined by the law and implementing rules

GENERAL ELIGIBILITY FOR FREE TUITION:
- Filipino citizen
- Passes admission requirements
- Maintains the required academic standing
- Enrolled in an undergraduate degree program
- Has not yet earned a bachelor's degree

WHAT YOU MAY STILL PAY (varies):
- Replacement of school ID or handbook
- Certain optional items (uniform, books)
- Personal expenses (transportation, meals)
- Graduate-school tuition (RA 10931 may not apply to graduate programs)

SCHOLARSHIPS & FINANCIAL AID may include:
- Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) for living-cost stipends
- Government scholarships
- Private scholarships
- Academic merit scholarships

For the current fee schedule, the precise list of fees covered/not covered, graduate-program tuition rates, and verified Cashier's Office contact details, please check www.cvsu.edu.ph or contact your campus Cashier's Office directly.

Sa ilalim ng RA 10931, libre ang tuition para sa mga qualified na Filipino students sa CvSU at iba pang SUC. May miscellaneous fees pa rin na maaaring bayaran (lab, library, ID, atbp.) na nag-iiba depende sa kurso at campus. Para sa tiyak na halaga, mangyaring magtanong sa Cashier's Office ng inyong campus o bisitahin ang www.cvsu.edu.ph. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
uniform_dress_code: CvSU enforces a basic dress code consistent with a professional learning environment — typically prohibiting overly revealing clothing, slippers in classrooms, and similar inappropriate attire. Specific departments may have stricter standards (e.g., lab gowns, closed shoes in engineering labs, PE attire, formal attire during department-specific activities). For everyday dress code rules and any campus-specific provisions, refer to the Student Handbook at your campus.

CvSU's dress code expects clothing appropriate to a professional learning environment. Some departments (engineering labs, PE, hospital practicum) have stricter requirements. See your Student Handbook.
May basic dress code ang CvSU na angkop sa propesyonal na pag-aaral. May mga departamento (engineering lab, PE, klinikal) na mas mahigpit. Tingnan ang Student Handbook.

veterinary_medicine: Veterinary education at CvSU is offered through the College of Veterinary Medicine and Biomedical Sciences. Admission to the Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) program typically requires completion of pre-veterinary undergraduate prerequisites and is competitive. Graduates take the Veterinary Medicine Licensure Examination administered by the PRC. For the current admission process, prerequisites, and program details, please refer to the college's page via https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

CvSU offers Veterinary Medicine through the College of Veterinary Medicine and Biomedical Sciences. DVM admission requires pre-vet prerequisites. Graduates take the PRC Veterinary licensure exam. Details: https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

vision_mission: Per the official University page, CvSU is mandated under Section 2 of Republic Act No. 8468 to "primarily provide advance instruction and professional training in agriculture, science and technology, education and other related fields, undertake research and extension services, and provide progressive leadership in these areas." Its vision is "The premier university in historic Cavite globally recognized for excellence in character development, academics, research, innovation and sustainable community engagement," and its mission states that "Cavite State University shall provide excellent, equitable and relevant educational opportunities in the arts, sciences and technology through quality instruction and responsive research and development activities. It shall produce professional, skilled and morally upright individuals for global competitiveness." The University's core values are Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service, and its quality policy commits to the highest standards of education, values its stakeholders, strives for continual improvement of its products and services, and upholds the University's tenets of Truth, Excellence, and Service to produce globally competitive and morally upright individuals. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/mandate-mission-vision-core-values-quality-policy/ The University's current strategy is CvSU ASCEND — Advancing Sustained Change and Excellence for National Development — its 2025-2028 Strategic Framework, implemented through the four-year IDEAL Plan: Inclusive and Accessible Education; Dynamic and Competitive Research and Innovation; Empowered Communities and Stronger Partnership; Accountable and Client-Centered Governance; and Long-Lasting Sustainable Resource Generation — as CvSU continues its journey toward becoming a Premier Global University.

CvSU's core values are Truth, Integrity, Excellence, and Service. Its vision: "The premier university in historic Cavite globally recognized for excellence in character development, academics, research, innovation and sustainable community engagement." Its mission: to provide excellent, equitable and relevant educational opportunities in the arts, sciences and technology through quality instruction and responsive research and development activities, producing professional, skilled and morally upright individuals for global competitiveness. CvSU is mandated under RA 8468 to primarily provide advance instruction and professional training in agriculture, science and technology, education and other related fields. visitor_accommodation:
VISITOR ACCOMMODATIONS (Public Affairs and Communications Office / PACO):

WHO MAY AVAIL: Any external agency or group (G2B, G2C, G2G).

REQUIREMENTS:
- Request letter addressed to the University President
- Accomplished Pre-visit Form (PACO-QF-05)

PROCESS:
1. Submit the letter of request to the Office of the University President (OUP).
2. PACO sends an acknowledgment receipt and provides the Pre-Visit Form.
3. Complete the Pre-Visit Form and submit it to PACO.
4. PACO coordinates with relevant offices (~4 days).
5. PACO confirms the final schedule.
6. Visit the university on the scheduled date — PACO and other offices accommodate the guests.

FEES: None.
PROCESSING TIME: ~5 days, 8 minutes (depends on length of visit).
Source: CvSU Citizen's Charter 2026 — PACO, p.51.

Para makapunta sa CvSU bilang visiting agency: magpadala ng request letter sa Office of the University President at sagutan ang Pre-Visit Form ng PACO. Mag-coordinate ang PACO sa mga office (around 4 days), kumpirmahin ang schedule, at puntahan sa nakatakdang araw. Libre. (Citizen's Charter 2026, p.51)
wifi_internet_access: Wi-Fi is typically available at CvSU campuses in libraries, computer laboratories, and certain common areas. For Wi-Fi access requests, password resets, or coverage issues, please contact the ICT/MIS Office.
